description_long: This is a vintage deep-sea diving helmet, reminiscent of early 20th-century designs used for underwater exploration. The helmet is constructed from dark, aged metal with a robust, rounded dome and a wide, flared base that would rest on the diver's shoulders. Prominent features include a large, circular front viewport with a metal grid, two smaller side windows, and a rear exhaust valve. The surface is adorned with various bolts, rivets, and fittings, giving it an industrial, utilitarian appearance. The metal has a slightly tarnished, bronze-like patina, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. There are no visible modern attachments, graphics, or branding, emphasizing its historical and functional character.
description: A vintage, bronze-colored deep-sea diving helmet with a gridded front viewport, side windows, and industrial fittings. The surface is aged and metallic, with a robust, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the oval side window with a metal grid and a protruding side fitting.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the rounded back, central exhaust valve, and absence of windows.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the opposite oval window with a grid and a curved pipe attachment.
description_view_3: Main front view featuring the large, circular gridded viewport and symmetrical fittings on the base.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Two oval side windows and one large circular front window, all covered with metal grids; no additional vents visible.
